name: brainframe-api

metadata.name: brainframe-api
metadata.summary: Provides a Python wrapper around the BrainFrame REST API.

documentation:
# BrainFrame Python API

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/brainframe-python-api/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://brainframe-python-api.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
```

```{image} https://circleci.com/gh/aotuai/brainframe-python.svg?style=svg
:alt: Publish Packages CI Status
:target: https://circleci.com/gh/aotuai/brainframe-python
```

This library is a Python wrapper around the BrainFrame REST API. It allows for
easy interaction with a BrainFrame server.

```python
from brainframe.api import BrainFrameAPI, bf_codecs

# Connect to the server
api = BrainFrameAPI("http://localhost")
# Create a new IP camera stream
stream_config = api.set_stream_configuration(
    bf_codecs.StreamConfiguration(
        name="New Stream",
        connection_type=bf_codecs.ConnType.IP_CAMERA,
        connection_options={"url": "rtsp://192.168.1.100"},
        runtime_options={},
    ))
api.start_analyzing(stream_config.id)
# Get results
analysis_results = api.get_latest_zone_statuses()
```

## Installation

The BrainFrame Python API is available on PyPI and can be installed with pip.
Install the version of the library that matches the version of BrainFrame that
you are using. For example, if you are using BrainFrame version 0.26.1:

```bash
pip3 install brainframe-api
```

## Documentation

Documentation for this library is available on [ReadTheDocs].

[readthedocs]: https://brainframe-python-api.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

documentation:
If not None, this is a unique tracking ID for the object. This ID
can be compared to detections from other frames to track the movement
of an object over time.

An object detected in a video frame. Detections can have attributes
attached to them that provide more information about the object as well as
other metadata like a unique tracking ID.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.condition_codecs.IntersectionPointType
documentation:
The point on a detection that must be inside a zone for the detection to
count as being inside the zone. The most commonly used intersection point
is BOTTOM, which counts a detection as being inside a zone if the bottom
center point of the detection in the zone.

A condition that must be met for an alarm to go off. Compares the number

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.name
documentation: The human-readable name of the video stream

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.premises_id
documentation:
The ID of the premises that this stream is a part of, or None if
this stream is not part of a premises

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.connection_type
documentation: The type of stream this configuration is describing

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.connection_options
documentation:
Contains configuration describing how this stream can be connected
to. The contents of this dict will depend on the connection type.

ConnType.IP_CAMERA:

: `url`: The URL of the IP camera to connect to

  `pipeline` (optional): A custom GStreamer pipeline to connect to
  the IP camera with

ConnType.FILE:

: `storage_id`: The storage ID of the video file to stream

  `transcode` (optional): If True, the video file will be
  transcoded before being streamed. By default, this value is True.

  `pipeline` (optional): A custom GStreamer pipeline to connect to
  the hosted stream of this video file with

ConnType.WEBCAM:

: `device_id`: The Video4Linux device ID of the webcam

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.runtime_options
documentation:
Key-value pairs of configuration information that changes the
runtime behavior of the video stream from its defaults

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.metadata
documentation:
Key-value pairs containing some custom user-defined set of data to
further describe the stream

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.id
documentation: The unique ID of this stream

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.ConnType.IP_CAMERA
documentation: A network camera that uses RTSP or HTTP

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.ConnType.WEBCAM
documentation: A webcam (usually USB)

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.config_codecs.StreamConfiguration.ConnType.FILE
documentation: An uploaded video file
Describes a video stream that BrainFrame may connect to and analyze.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.Zone

methods.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.Zone.get_alarm
documentation:
---
param alarm_id: The ID of the alarm to search in the alarm list for
return: |-
  The alarm with the given ID, or None if no alarm with that ID
  exists in this zone
---

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.Zone.FULL_FRAME_ZONE_NAME
documentation: The name of the default full-frame zone that exists on every stream

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.Zone.name
documentation: A friendly name for the zone

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.Zone.stream_id
documentation: The ID of the stream this zone is in

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.Zone.coords
documentation:
Coordinates that define the region the zone occupies. It is a list
of lists which are two elements in size. The coordinates are in pixels
where the top left of the frame is [0, 0].

Example: [[0, 0], [10, 10], [100, 500], [0, 500]]

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.Zone.alarms
documentation: All alarms that are attached to the zone

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.Zone.id
documentation: A unique identifier
The definition for a zone. It is a non-convex polygon or a line.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus

methods.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus.detection_within_counts
documentation:
The current count of each class type detected in the video.

:returns: A dict whose keys are class names and whose values are the
    count for that class name

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus.zone
documentation: The zone that this status pertains to

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus.tstamp
documentation:
The time at which this ZoneStatus was created as a Unix timestamp in
seconds

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus.total_entered
documentation:
A dict of key-value pairs indicating how many objects have exited the
zone. The key is the class name, and the value is the count.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus.total_exited
documentation:
A set of key-value pairs indicating how many objects have exited the
zone. The key is the object type, and the value is the count.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus.within
documentation: A list of all detections within the zone

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus.entering
documentation: A list of all detections that entered the zone this frame

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus.exiting
documentation: A list of all detections that have exited the zone this frame

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.zone_codecs.ZoneStatus.alerts
documentation: A list of all active alerts for the zone at this frame
The current status of everything going on inside a zone.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.CapsuleOption

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.CapsuleOption.type
documentation: The data type of this option's value

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.CapsuleOption.default
documentation: The default value for this option

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.CapsuleOption.constraints
documentation:
Describes the range of valid values for this option. The content of
this dict depends on the type field.

OptionType.FLOAT:

: `max_val`: The maximum valid float value

  `min_val`: The minimum valid float value

OptionType.INT:

: `max_val`: The maximum valid int value

  `min_val`: The minimum valid int value

OptionType.ENUM:

: `choices`: A list of strings. The option's value must be one of
  these strings.

OptionType.BOOL:

: This object has no constraints.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.CapsuleOption.description
documentation: A human-readable description of the capsule's capabilities

inner_classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.CapsuleOption.Type
documentation: The data type of a capsule option

A single configuration option for a capsule. Defines what type of option
it is and its potential values.

There are two kinds of capsule options. Stream capsule options apply only
to the stream they are attached to. Global capsule options apply to all
streams, but are overridden by stream capsule options.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.size
documentation:
Describes the amount of DetectionNodes the node either takes in as
input or provides as output

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.detections
documentation:
A list of detection class names, like “person” or “vehicle”. A
DetectionNode that meets this description must have a class name that
is present in this list.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.attributes
documentation:
Key-value pairs whose key is the classification type and whose value
is a list of possible attributes. A DetectionNode that meets this
description must have a classification for each classification type
listed here.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.encoded
documentation: If True, the DetectionNode must be encoded to meet this description

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.tracked
documentation: If True, the DetectionNode must be tracked to meet this description

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.extra_data
documentation:
A list of keys in a NodeDescription's extra_data. A DetectionNode
that meets this description must have extra data for each name listed
here.

inner_classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.Size

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.Size.NONE
documentation:
Input: The capsule takes nothing as input, like for an object
detector.

Output: Capsules cannot have a NONE output.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.Size.SINGLE
documentation:
Input: The capsule takes a single DetectionNode as input, like for a
classifier.

Output: The capsule provides a single modified DetectionNode as output,
like for a classifier.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.NodeDescription.Size.ALL
documentation:
Input: The capsule takes all instances of a class as input, like for
a tracker.

Output: The capsule provides all instances of a class as output, like
for a detector.

Describes the amount of DetectionNodes a capsule takes as input or
provides as output.

A description of a DetectionNode, used by capsules to define what kinds
of inputs and outputs a capsule uses.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.Capsule.description
documentation: A human-readable description of what the capsule does

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.Capsule.input_type
documentation: Describes the type of inference data that this capsule takes as input

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.Capsule.output_type
documentation: Describes the type of inference data that this capsule produces

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.Capsule.capability
documentation:
A NodeDescription which describes what this capsule does to its
input. It is the difference between the input and output
NodeDescriptions. This field is useful for inspecting a capsule to find
what it can do.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_codecs.capsule_codecs.Capsule.options
documentation: A dict describing the configurable options of this capsule
Metadata on a loaded capsule.

The timeout to use for this request

return

License info

documentation:
Uploads a license key to the server and applies it.

A base64-encoded string of license data

The timeout to use for this request

The ID of the premises to get

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

The premises

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.premises.PremisesStubMixin.set_premises
documentation:
Update or create a premises. If the Premises doesn't exist, the
premises.id must be None. An initialized Premises with an ID will be
returned.

Premises are more often created using the StreamGateway client tool.

```
param premises

A Premises object

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

Premises, initialized with an ID
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.premises.PremisesStubMixin.delete_premises
documentation:
Delete a premises and the streams and data connected to it.

```
param premises_id

The ID of the premises to delete

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request
```
Provides stubs for calling APIs to get, set, and delete zones.

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.cloud_users.CloudUsersStubMixIn.get_current_cloud_user
documentation:

```
param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

Information on the cloud user, or None if no user is logged in

StreamConfiguration

If specified, only encodings attached to this

attached to this identity will be returned

All class names from encodings that match this filter

The encoding ID to get an encoding for

classes.name: brainframe.api.stubs.zone_statuses.ZoneStatusStubMixin

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.zone_statuses.ZoneStatusStubMixin.get_latest_zone_statuses
documentation:
This method gets all of the latest processed zone statuses for every
zone and for every stream.

All active streams will have a key in the output dict.

```
param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

A dict whose keys are stream IDs and whose value is another
dict. This nested dict's keys are zone names and their value is the
ZoneStatus for that zone.
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.zone_statuses.ZoneStatusStubMixin.get_zone_status_stream
documentation:
Streams ZoneStatus results from the server as they are produced.

All active streams will have a key in the output dict.

```
param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

A generator that outputs dicts whose keys are stream IDs and
whose value is another dict. This nested dict's keys are zone names
and their value is the ZoneStatus for that zone.
```
Provides stubs for calling APIs to get zone statuses.

classes.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.get_capsule
documentation:
---
param name: The name of the capsule to get
param timeout: The timeout to use for this request
return: Capsule with the given name
---

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.get_capsules
documentation:
---
param timeout: The timeout to use for this request
return: All available capsules
---

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.load_capsule
documentation:
Loads and initializes a capsule from capsule data in storage.

```
param storage_id

The ID of the raw capsule data in storage

param source_path

If available, the path to the source code on the
development machine can be provided. Doing so allows stack traces
to point to the correct source location.

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

The loaded capsule
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.upload_and_load_capsule
documentation:
Uploads capsule data to storage, then loads and initializes a
capsule from it. This is a utility method that combines the
functionality of `new_storage` and `load_capsule`.

```
param data

The data to store, either as bytes or as a file-like

param source_path

If available, the path to the source code on the
development machine can be provided. Doing so allows stack traces
to point to the correct source location.

param timeout

The timeout to use for each request

return

The loaded capsule
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.unload_capsule
documentation:
Unloads a capsule and deletes its data from storage. This method may
only be used with capsules that were loaded through the REST API.

```
param capsule_name

The name of the capsule to unload

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.get_capsule_option_vals
documentation:
Gets the current values for every capsule option. See the
documentation for the CapsuleOption codec for more info about global
and stream level options and how they interact.

```
param capsule_name

The capsule to find options for

param stream_id

The ID of the stream. If this value is None, then the
global options are returned for that capsule

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

A dict where the key is the option name and the value is the
option's current value
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.set_capsule_option_vals
documentation:
Sets option values for a capsule.

```
param capsule_name

The name of the capsule whose options to set

param stream_id

The ID of the stream, if these are stream-level
options. If this value is None, then the global options are set

param option_vals

A dict where the key is the name of the option to
set, and the value is the value to set that option to

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.patch_capsule_option_vals
documentation:
Patches option values for a capsule. Only the provided options are
changed. To unset an option, provide that option with a value of None.

```
param capsule_name

The name of the capsule whose options to set

param stream_id

The ID of the stream, if these are stream-level
options. If this value is None, then the global options are set

param option_vals

A dict where the key is the name of the option to
set, and the value is the value to set that option to

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.is_capsule_active
documentation:
Returns True if the capsule is active. If a capsule is not marked as
active, it will not run. Like capsule options, this can be configured
globally and on a per-stream level.

```
param capsule_name

The name of the capsule to get activity for

param stream_id

The ID of the stream, if you want the per-stream
active setting

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

True if the capsule is active
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.capsules.CapsuleStubMixin.set_capsule_active
documentation:
Sets whether or not the capsule is active. If a capsule is active, it
will be run on frames.

```
param capsule_name

The name of the capsule to set activity for

param stream_id

The ID of the stream, if you want to set the
per-stream active setting

param active

True if the capsule should be set to active

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request
```
Provides stubs to call APIs to inspect and configure capsules.

Stores the given data.

The data to store, either as bytes or as a file-like

param mime_type

The MIME type of the data

The timeout to use for this request

return

The storage ID

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.storage.StorageStubMixin.new_storage_as_image
documentation:
Stores the given image data, and inspects it to figure out what the
MIME type of the data.

```
param data

The image data to store

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

The storage ID
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.storage.StorageStubMixin.delete_storage
documentation:
Deletes the storage object with the given ID. Deleting storage
objects that are in use, like for an encoding, is not recommended.

```
param storage_id

The ID of the storage object to delete

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request
```
Provides stubs to call APIs for managing binary blob storage.

classes.name: brainframe.api.stubs.process_image.ProcessImageStubMixIn

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.process_image.ProcessImageStubMixIn.process_image
documentation:
Process a single image using the given configuration.
\:param img_bgr: The image to process
\:param capsule_names: The capsule names to enable while processing the

> image

```
param option_vals

Capsule option values, where the key is a capsule
name and the value is a dict with key-value pairs for each option
and its corresponding value. Any specified options will override
the global option values for that capsule

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

All detections in the image
```
Provides stubs to call APIs that run processing on a single frame.

classes.name: brainframe.api.stubs.oauth2.OAuth2StubMixIn

The OAuth2 info

classes.name: brainframe.api.stubs.identities.IdentityStubMixin

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.identities.IdentityStubMixin.get_identity
documentation:
Gets the identity with the given ID.

```
param identity_id

The ID of the identity to get

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

Identity
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.identities.IdentityStubMixin.get_identities
documentation:
Returns all identities from the server.

```
param unique_name

If provided, identities will be filtered by only
those who have the given unique name

param encoded_for_class

If provided, identities will be filtered for
only those that have been encoded at least once for the given class

param search

If provided, only identities that in some way contain
the given search query are returned. This is intended for UI search
features, and as such the specific semantics of how the search is
performed are subject to change.

param limit

If provided, the number of returned identities is limited
to this value.

param offset

The offset to start limiting results from. This is only
useful when providing a limit.

param sort_by

If provided, the results will be sorted by the given
configuration

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

A list of identities, and the total number of identities that
fit this criteria, ignoring pagination (the limit and offset)
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.identities.IdentityStubMixin.set_identity
documentation:
Updates or creates an identity. If the identity does not already
exist, identity.id must be None. The returned identity will have an
assigned ID.

```
param identity

The identity to save or create

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

the saved identity
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.identities.IdentityStubMixin.delete_identity
documentation:
Deletes the identity with the given ID.

```
param identity_id

The ID of the identity to delete

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.identities.IdentityStubMixin.new_identity_image
documentation:
Saves and encodes an image under the identity with the given ID.

```
param identity_id

Identity to associate the image with

param class_name

The type of object this image shows and should be
encoded for

param storage_id

The ID of the image in storage to encode

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.identities.IdentityStubMixin.new_identity_vector
documentation:
Saves the given vector under the identity with the given ID. In this
case, a vector is simply a list of one or more numbers that describe
some object in an image.

```
param identity_id

Identity to associate the vector with

param class_name

The type of object this vector describes

param vector

The vector to save

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

The vector ID
```

Provides stubs to call APIs that create and update identities, as well
as add new examples of the identity in image or vector form.

classes.name: brainframe.api.stubs.analysis.AnalysisStubMixin

Starts analysis on this stream.

The ID of the stream to start analysis on

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.analysis.AnalysisStubMixin.stop_analyzing
documentation:
Stops analysis on this stream.

```
param stream_id

The ID of the stream to stop analysis on

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

True or False if the server was able to start analysis on that

restrictions.
```

documentation:
Check if this stream is being analyzed

The ID of the stream to check

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

True or False if the stream is being analyzed
```

classes.name: brainframe.api.stubs.alerts.AlertStubMixin

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.alerts.AlertStubMixin.get_alert
documentation:
Gets the alert with the given ID.

```
param alert_id

The ID of the alert to get

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

The alert
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.alerts.AlertStubMixin.get_alerts
documentation:
Gets all alerts that match a query

```
param stream_id

The ID of the stream to get alerts for

param zone_id

The ID of the zone to get alerts for

param alarm_id

The ID of the alarm to get alerts for

param verification

The verification states of the alerts

param limit

The maximum number of alerts to return. If None, no limit
will be applied

param offset

The offset from the most recent alerts to return. This
is only useful when providing a limit.

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

A list of alerts, and the total number of alerts that
fit this criteria, ignoring pagination (the limit and offset)
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.alerts.AlertStubMixin.set_alert_verification
documentation:
Sets an alert verified as True or False.

```
param alert_id

The ID of the alert to set

param verified_as

Set verification to True or False

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

The modified Alert
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.alerts.AlertStubMixin.get_alert_frame
documentation:
Returns the frame saved for this alert, or None if no frame is
recorded for this alert.

```
param alert_id

The ID of the alert to get a frame for.

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

return

The image as loaded by OpenCV, or None
```

Provides stubs for calling APIs related to controlling and getting
alerts.

classes.name: brainframe.api.status_receiver.StatusReceiver

methods.name: brainframe.api.status_receiver.StatusReceiver.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new StatusReceiver. Consider using the StatusReceiver that
comes included with the API object with get_status_receiver instead.

```
param api

Used to communicate with the BrainFrame server
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.status_receiver.StatusReceiver.add_listener
documentation:
---
param listener: |-
  Called when new data is received from the zone status
  stream
---

methods.name: brainframe.api.status_receiver.StatusReceiver.latest_statuses
documentation:
Returns the latest cached list of ZoneStatuses for that stream_id,
or any empty dict if none are cached

methods.name: brainframe.api.status_receiver.StatusReceiver.close
documentation: Close the status receiving thread

methods.name: brainframe.api.status_receiver.StatusReceiver._run
documentation:
Opens a connection with BrainFrame to receive ZoneStatus objects.
Then, alerts any event handlers of new objects.

class_variables.name: brainframe.api.status_receiver.StatusReceiver._ZONE_STATUS_STREAM_TIMEOUT
documentation:
The timeout in seconds before reinitializing a connection to the server. 
If none, the zone status stream will wait forever even if there are no 
results, making the thread potentially never check if it should be 
closing.

A simple wrapper around API.get_zone_status_stream that handles reading
from the stream in a separate thread and notifies listeners when new data
is received.

The StatusReceiver corresponding to an API object can be retrieved using
get_status_receiver. This is usually preferred over creating a
StatusReceiver manually.

functions.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors._server_origin_error
documentation:
A class decorator. Registers the given class so that it will be used
when an error with a kind equal to the class's name is provided as a
response. Used only for Errors that can originate on the BrainFrame server.

```
param kind

If provided, this class will be associated with this kind
instead of using the class's name as the kind
```

variables.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.kind_to_error_type
documentation: Maps error kinds to their corresponding error type.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.BaseAPIError
documentation: All API errors subclass this error.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.UnknownError
documentation: Something unexpected happened. The server may be in an invalid state.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.StreamConfigNotFoundError
documentation: A StreamConfiguration specified by the client could not be found.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.ZoneNotFoundError
documentation: A Zone specified by the client could not be found.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.PremisesNotFoundError
documentation: A Zone specified by the client could not be found.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.ZoneNotDeletableError
documentation: A client tried to delete a default Zone

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.ZoneNotEditableError
documentation: A client tried to edit a read-only Zone

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.AlertNotFoundError
documentation: An Alert specified by the client could not be found.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.InvalidSyntaxError
documentation: The syntax of the request could not be parsed.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.InvalidFormatError
documentation: The request was parsed, but some value within the request is invalid.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.NotImplementedInAPIError
documentation: The client requested something that is not currently implemented.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.StreamNotOpenedError
documentation: A stream failed to open when it was required to.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.DuplicateStreamSourceError
documentation:
There was an attempted to create a stream configuration with the same
source as an existing one.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.DuplicateZoneNameError
documentation:
There was an attempt to make a zone with the same name as another zone
within the same stream.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.DuplicateIdentityNameError
documentation:
There was an attempt to create a new identity with the same name as
another identity.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.NoDetectorForClassError
documentation: There was an attempt to use a class name that is not detectable.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.NoEncoderForClassError
documentation:
There was an attempt to create an identity for a class that is not
encodable.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.IdentityNotFoundError
documentation: An identity specified by the client could not be found.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.ImageNotFoundForIdentityError
documentation:
An image specified by the client could not be found for the specified
identity.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.InvalidImageTypeError
documentation: An image could not be decoded by OpenCV

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.AnalysisLimitExceededError
documentation:
There was an attempt to start analysis on a stream, but the maximum
amount of streams that may have analysis run on them at once has already
been reached.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.NoDetectionsInImageError
documentation:
There was an attempt to encode an image with no objects of the given
class in the frame.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.TooManyDetectionsInImageError
documentation:
There was an attempt to encode an image with more than one object of the
given class in the frame, causing ambiguity on which one to encode.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.ImageAlreadyEncodedError
documentation:
There was an attempt to encode an image that has already been encoded
for a given identity and a given class.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.DuplicateVectorError
documentation:
There was an attempt to add a vector that already exists for the given
identity and class.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.FrameNotFoundForAlertError
documentation: There was an attempt to get a frame for an alert that has no frame.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.CapsuleNotFoundError
documentation: There was an attempt to reference a capsule that does not exist.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.InvalidCapsuleOptionError
documentation:
The provided capsule options do not work for the given capsule. This
could be because the option does not exist or the value for that option
doesn't fit the constraints.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.StorageNotFoundError
documentation: There was an attempt to access a storage object that does not exist.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.ZoneAlarmNotFoundError
documentation: There was an attempt to access a zone alarm that does not exist.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.InvalidRuntimeOptionError
documentation:
There was an attempt to set a runtime option that is not supported or is
of the wrong type.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.EncodingNotFoundError
documentation: There was an attempt to access an encoding that does not exist.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.UnauthorizedError
documentation: There was an attempt to access the API without proper authorization.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.InvalidSessionError
documentation:
There was an attempt to access the API with an invalid session ID,
either because the session expired or no session with that ID has ever
existed. The client should authenticate again to get a new session.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.VectorTooLongError
documentation:
The provided encoding vector is longer than the maximum allowed
length.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.UserNotFoundError
documentation: There was an attempt to access a user by ID that does not exist.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.InsufficientRoleError
documentation: A user attempted an operation that they don't have permission to do.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.DuplicateUsernameError
documentation: The requested username already exists.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.AdminMustExistError
documentation: There was an attempt to delete the only remaining admin account.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.LicenseRequiredError
documentation:
There was an attempt to access a resource that requires an active
license while no valid license is loaded.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.LicenseExpiredError
documentation: There was an attempt to upload a license that is expired.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.LicenseInvalidError
documentation: There was an attempt to upload a license that is in an invalid format.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.RemoteConnectionError
documentation:
The server encountered an error while connecting to a remote resource
that is required for the requested operation.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.InvalidCapsuleError
documentation: The provided capsule could not be loaded.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.IncompatibleCapsuleError
documentation: The provided capsule is not compatible with this version of BrainFrame.

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.UnauthorizedTokensError
documentation: The provided tokens do not correctly authorize a BrainFrame Cloud user

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.CloudUserNotFoundError
documentation: No cloud user is logged in

classes.name: brainframe.api.bf_errors.ServerNotReadyError
documentation:
The client was able to communicate with the server, but the server had
not completed startup or was in an invalid state

functions.name: brainframe.api.stubs.base_stub._make_api_error
documentation:
Makes the corresponding error for this response.

Pass either a requests.Response with a status code, or an exception

```
param resp

The HTTP response to inspect for info

param exception

An exception during the request

return

An error that can be thrown describing this failure
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.base_stub.BaseStub._get_json
documentation:
Send a GET request to the given URL and parse the result as JSON.

```
param api_url

The /api/blah/blah to append to the base_url

param timeout

The timeout to use for this request

param params

The "query_string" to add to the url. In the format
of a dict, {"key": "value", ...} key and val must be a string

return

The response, parsed with JSON
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stubs.base_stub.BaseStub._send_request
documentation:
Sends a request to the server. This method is mocked out in unit
tests.

```
param request

The request to send

return

The response data
```

A base class for API stubs: Classes that provide methods which call the
API to provide some functionality. Included are methods to make
communicating with the API easier.

classes.name: brainframe.api.stub.BrainFrameAPI

methods.name: brainframe.api.stub.BrainFrameAPI.__init__
documentation:
---
param credentials: |-
  The username and password as a tuple. Used to
  authenticate with the server. If None, no authentication
  information will be provided.
param server_url: |-
  The URL of the BrainFrame instance to connect to. If
  None, it needs to be set later with set_url before use
---

methods.name: brainframe.api.stub.BrainFrameAPI.version
documentation:
---
return: The current BrainFrame version in the format X.Y.Z
---

methods.name: brainframe.api.stub.BrainFrameAPI.wait_for_server_initialization
documentation:
Waits for the server to be ready to handle requests.

```
param timeout

The maximum amount of time, in seconds, to wait for the
server to start. If None, this method will wait indefinitely.
```

methods.name: brainframe.api.stub.BrainFrameAPI.close
documentation: Clean up the API. It may no longer be used after this call.
Provides access to BrainFrame API endpoints.
